_meta.purpose: Decisions made by the agentic system and why — separate from product decisions in docs/DECISIONS_LOG.md. This file tracks AGENTIC and ARCHITECTURAL decisions.

product_decisions.note: Product decisions live in docs/DECISIONS_LOG.md. Key ones mirrored here for agent context.

summary.decision: Full isolation from Connsura runtime and infrastructure
6-char alphanumeric accessCode as organizer credential (no OAuth/JWT)
summary.consequence: Simple onboarding; tradeoff is no password recovery (access code IS the credential)
Scannable as URL or raw ID; users can verify ticket state at /t/:id

summary.decision: C:\Users\yonat\Downloads\QR_Tickets is the ONLY active working repo
summary.consequence: OneDrive copy is archived/read-only unless explicitly requested

summary.decision: TicketRequest lifecycle with PENDING_VERIFICATION → APPROVED/REJECTED (renamed from PENDING_PAYMENT 2026-03-17)
summary.consequence: Payment stays off-platform; organizer controls issuance

summary.decision: Store salesWindowStart/End as HH:MM strings (24h), not UTC DateTimes
summary.consequence: Timezone-portable; comparison done in server local time

summary.decision: Backend serves OG meta tags for social crawlers; nginx routes by User-Agent
summary.consequence: Social previews work; no change to React SPA routing for real users

agentic_decisions.title: Fix chat attachment path by moving to existing volume rather than adding a new volume mount
agentic_decisions.context: BUG-011: chat attachments were stored at /app/storage/private/chat which was outside the Docker volume. Two options: (a) change the path constant to /app/uploads/private/chat, or (b) add a second volume mount in docker-compose.yml for /app/storage.
agentic_decisions.decision: Option (a): change CHAT_STORAGE_ROOT constant in chatAttachmentService.js to resolve under /app/uploads. No infrastructure change required. Reuses existing volume mount.
alternatives_rejected: Add second volume mount (/app/storage) — adds infrastructure complexity, requires deploy script change, more surface area
agentic_decisions.consequence: Chat attachments now survive container rebuilds. Existing attachments at the old path (pre-fix) were already ephemeral and no migration needed.
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: If /app/uploads grows large and needs to be split, a separate volume for chat can be added then

agentic_decisions.title: Use existing otpLimiter for recover-organizer-code; create separate contactLimiter for contact form
agentic_decisions.context: BUG-012: recovery/contact endpoints had no rate limiting. Options: (a) reuse existing limiters, (b) create new dedicated limiters.
agentic_decisions.decision: Reuse otpLimiter (5/hr) for recover-organizer-code (same threat model as other OTP endpoints). Create new contactLimiter (3/hr) for contact form — stricter because contact form sends email to admin and one submission per hour is the normal case.
alternatives_rejected: Single global limiter for all public endpoints — too broad, would affect unrelated endpoints
agentic_decisions.consequence: All 7 unauthenticated email-sending endpoints are now rate limited. contactLimiter is tighter (3/hr) reflecting the lower expected volume of legitimate contact form submissions.
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: If legitimate users hit contactLimiter too often, raise to 5/hr

agentic_decisions.title: Run npm audit fix without --force on both packages
agentic_decisions.context: BUG-014: 8 HIGH vulnerabilities in backend, 2 in frontend. npm audit fix without --force only applies safe (semver-compatible) fixes.
agentic_decisions.decision: Run npm audit fix (no --force) only. This updates transitive dependencies in package-lock.json without changing declared versions in package.json. Result: 0 vulnerabilities in both packages.
alternatives_rejected: npm audit fix --force — could introduce breaking major version changes; not needed since safe fix resolved all issues
agentic_decisions.consequence: 0 vulnerabilities. package.json unchanged. Only package-lock.json updated with 18 transitive package version changes.
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: Run npm audit on every CI pipeline run (TASK-006)

agentic_decisions.title: Establish /agentic/ as the persistent memory layer inside the repo
agentic_decisions.context: The system had no executable agentic infrastructure — only documentation stubs. A repo-local memory layer enables cross-session continuity without external tooling.
agentic_decisions.decision: Create /agentic/ with JSON memory files + markdown behavior files. JSON for machine-readable state; markdown for human-readable behavior specs.
alternatives_rejected: External vector DB — too much infrastructure for current scale
Plain markdown memory files only — less structured, harder for agents to parse and update
Keeping memory in .claude/ only — not visible to humans reviewing the repo
agentic_decisions.consequence: Every session can load context from JSON files. Memory is version-controlled alongside code.
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: If memory files become large (>500 lines each), consider splitting by sub-domain

agentic_decisions.title: Phase ordering: foundation before product features
agentic_decisions.context: The system has 0% test coverage, no CI, no structured logging. Building AI features on this base would be building on sand.
agentic_decisions.decision: Phase 1 = engineering + agentic foundation only. No product features until CI, tests, and logging are in place.
agentic_decisions.consequence: Product AI features (approval assist, analytics) deferred to Phase 3 minimum
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: After TASK-006 (CI) and TASK-007+008 (critical path tests) are complete

agentic_decisions.title: Use JSON + Markdown hybrid for agentic memory files
agentic_decisions.context: Pure JSON is machine-readable but poor for complex behavioral instructions. Pure markdown is human-readable but hard to query programmatically.
agentic_decisions.decision: Use JSON for state (memory_*.json files) and Markdown for behavior (agent_loop.md, roles.md, etc.).
agentic_decisions.consequence: Agents load JSON for facts, load Markdown for behavioral instructions
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: If scripted automation needs to read memory, JSON is already machine-parseable

agentic_decisions.title: Do not rewrite working product code during agentic foundation setup
agentic_decisions.context: The product is live and functional. Agentic foundation work must not introduce regressions.
agentic_decisions.decision: Foundation files are all new files or prompt rewrites. No modifications to backend/ or frontend/ in Phase 1.
agentic_decisions.consequence: Phase 1 is zero-risk to the live product
agentic_decisions.review_trigger: Phase 2 tasks begin touching backend code
